description: Tax-aligned chart of accounts for an individual (Form 1040) whose books aggregate passthrough and investment activity. Unlike an operating entity, a 1040 'CoA' is organized by SCHEDULE: each account maps to the schedule/line where it lands. Passthrough K-1 items keep their character (ordinary vs rental vs portfolio). Numbering: 4xxx income by schedule, 5xxx passthrough losses, 9xxx non-reportable.

accounts.name: Schedule E — S-corp/Partnership Income (K-1)
accounts.note: One account per K-1 entity; keeps ordinary vs rental character.

accounts.name: Schedule D — Long-Term Capital Gain (8949)
accounts.note: e.g. stock sale — via Form 8949 Box; keep short- vs long-term separate.

accounts.name: Schedule E — S-corp/Partnership Loss (K-1)
accounts.note: Passthrough loss — stays SIGNED (negative) into Schedule E; do not take magnitude.

accounts.name: Non-Reportable / Personal
accounts.note: Personal draws, transfers, non-taxable items — never on the return.
